sections.paragraph:
an unused root.-

(1) to have the mouth full, to swallow down. (Arab. فأم to have the mouth full of food; Eth. ተፈእመ፡ to have in the mouth a morsel, lump, ፈእም፡ morsel, lump; περιστόμιον? It is one of the roots ending in m which express sounds uttered with the mouth shut. Cognate is فهم to understand, prop. to be imbued with.) Hence פּוּם (for פְּאוּם) mouth.

(2) Arab. فَيءِمَ to be fat (of the same stock appears to be the Sanscr. pîna, fat, πιμελής, πιμελή, opimus, pinguis). Hence n. פִּימָה.

I.
